sections_cited: section 133 of the Income Tax Ordinance

chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI ITRA 324 of 2017 ___________________________________________________________ DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S) ___________________________________________________________ 1. For orders on office objection No.19. 2. For orders on CMA No.411/2017. 3. For hearing of main case. 12.01.2026 Mr. Muhammad Aqeel Qureshi, advocate for the applicant. Matter is pending since 2017 without any progress. On 03.10.2017, following order was passed: “03.10.2017 Mr. Mohammad Aqeel Qureshi, advocate for the applicant requests for time to prepare the case. On his request adjourned to a date in office.” On 16.12.2025, following order was passed: “16.12.2025 Mr. Muhammad Aqeel Qureshi, advocate for applicant. Learned counsel seeks time to assist the Court. At his request, to come up after winter vacations.” Even today learned counsel is unable to assist. It appears that the applicant is not interested in the present proceedings. Accordingly, in view of judgment of CIR vs. Rafeh Limited reported as PLD 2020 SC 518, the reference application is dismissed for non-prosecution. A copy of this decision may be sent under the seal of this Court and the signature of the Registrar to the learned Appellate Tribunal, as required per section 133 of the Income Tax Ordinance, 2001. Judge Judge Khuhro/PS
